name: Piccolo
description: Stackless Lua VM implemented in pure Rust with focus on safe sandboxing

license.comment: Dual-licensed under MIT or CC0 1.0 (public domain)

language-family.comment: Aims to emulate PUC-Rio Lua behavior

first-release.comment: v0.2.0 released October 2023; project resumed after 4-year pause

closures.comment: Real closures with proper upvalue handling

error-handling.comment: Lua pcall/error for exceptions; Rust API returns Result types

async-await.comment: No native async/await in Lua scripts; has Rust-side async Sequence mechanism

iterators-generators.comment: Coroutines including yielding transparent to Rust callbacks

standard-library.comment: Most stdlib unimplemented: io, file, os, package, string, table, utf8 are missing or sparse

execution-strategy.comment: Compiles to bytecode executed by stackless VM

compilation-model.comment: Can compile at runtime or use precompiled bytecode

hot-reloading.comment: Can load new scripts without restarting interpreter

repl-support.comment: Includes rustyline as dev dependency for REPL

source-maps.comment: Runtime errors map back to source locations

execution-speed.comment: Goal is to not be fundamentally slower than PUC-Rio Lua; optimization incomplete

memory-overhead.comment: Zero-cost Gc pointers (machine pointer sized); designed for embedding

garbage-collection.comment: Cycle-detecting incremental garbage collector via gc-arena crate

api-safety.comment: Zero-cost Gc pointers usable from safe Rust; unsafe contained internally

idiomatic-api.comment: Good Rust patterns (Executor, Context, traits) but only 10% documented; experimental API

serde-integration.comment: Via piccolo-util crate for Rust type to Lua table conversion

async-compatible.comment: Advanced async Sequence mechanism with shadow stack for holding GC values across await points

function-registration.comment: Callback system for registering Rust functions; no derive macros

type-mapping.comment: Manual conversion needed; serde support via piccolo-util

wasm-compatible.comment: Pure Rust but WASM compatibility not documented or verified

blank-slate-mode.comment: Can start with empty context; host controls all available symbols

memory-sandboxing.comment: Scripts cannot access arbitrary host memory

cpu-limits.comment: Fuel-based execution control for bounding CPU usage

memory-limits.comment: Memory tracking and limits via gc-arena

syscall-restrictions.comment: No io/os/file libraries by default; host must explicitly provide capabilities

capability-security.comment: Host explicitly grants capabilities; designed for DoS resilience

default-deny.comment: Scripts have no capabilities by default; designed to safely sandbox untrusted scripts

share-data.comment: Gc pointers for references; userdata participates in garbage collection

custom-types.comment: Garbage collected userdata with safe downcasting

method-syntax.comment: Lua colon syntax supported via metatables

callbacks-events.comment: Robust callback system with sequencing callbacks

package-manager.comment: No package ecosystem; package library not implemented

editor-support.comment: Standard Lua editor tooling works for syntax

example-projects.comment: Designed for game scripting and plugin systems; sandboxing focus

maintenance-status.comment: Recently unpaused after 4 years; active development through 2024

documentation-quality.comment: Only 10% of crate documented; minimal examples but good README

maturity.comment: Pre-1.0; warns of frequent API breakage; most stdlib unimplemented

primary-use-cases.comment: Focus on safe sandboxing of untrusted scripts with DoS resilience
